# hang:out

> hang:out is a free Android app — an availability radar for friends with
> mismatched schedules. It reads each person's calendar on their own device,
> reduces it to plain free/busy time ranges, and shows the group the one
> thing group chats can't: when you're all free. One tap turns that window
> into a plan with live RSVPs.

The privacy invariant: friends only ever see free or busy — never event
names, notes, or details. Raw calendar events never leave the phone.
Free/busy data is friends-only, enforced with row-level security; deleting
an account immediately removes everything server-side.

Status: v1 is in a closed test on Google Play; public launch next. The
site collects a waitlist (one email at launch). iOS is planned after
Android. Tagline: "Find the overlap."

## Pages

- [Home](https://the.hang-out.app/): what hang:out does — shifts and
  calendar in, per-day free counts, one-tap plans with live RSVP,
  rare-overlap alerts, FAQ, waitlist
- [Privacy policy](https://the.hang-out.app/privacy): on-device calendar
  reduction, what's stored (Supabase, EU), friends-only access, deletion
- [Terms of Service](https://the.hang-out.app/terms)
- [Acceptable Use Policy](https://the.hang-out.app/acceptable-use)
- [Delete your account](https://the.hang-out.app/delete-account): in-app
  path and email fallback

## Contact

- Email: hello@hang-out.app
